Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 574 entries across all 19 seed CSV files

Comprehensive additions covering:
- Controls: sector-specific (automotive, maritime, aviation, railway, medical,
  telecom, nuclear, water), country-specific frameworks, NIST SPs, quantum/PQC,
  CISA frameworks, NERC CIP details, ISA/IEC 62443 parts, financial controls
- Standards: TLS/DTLS/HTTP/QUIC protocols, auth protocols (SAML/OAuth/OIDC/FIDO2),
  crypto standards (FIPS 197-205, PKCS, X.509), email security (DMARC/SPF/DKIM),
  DNS security, threat intel formats (STIX/TAXII/Sigma/YARA), SBOM formats,
  vulnerability scoring (CVSS/EPSS/SSVC), SCAP/OSCAL, detection formats
- Regulations: 40+ country privacy laws (MENA, LATAM, APAC, Africa), 15+ US state
  privacy acts, EU digital regulation suite (AI Act, CRA, DSA, DMA, Data Act),
  export controls (ITAR/EAR/Wassenaar), sector laws (HIPAA/GLBA/SOX/FISMA)
- References: OWASP guides (ASVS/SAMM/MASTG/WSTG), annual threat reports
  (DBIR/M-Trends/CrowdStrike GTR), CIS Benchmarks, DISA STIGs, MITRE D3FEND
- Threat intel: VirusTotal, Recorded Future, Mandiant, GreyNoise, Censys, Shodan,
  OpenCTI, MISP, TheHive, abuse.ch feeds
- Vulndbs: VulnCheck, Snyk, Grype, Trivy, OSV.dev, deps.dev, national CVE DBs
- Exploits: Metasploit, Nuclei, C2 frameworks, LOLBAS/GTFOBins, Atomic Red Team
- Scanners: Semgrep, CodeQL, IaC scanners, CSPM tools, OPA/Kyverno, Falco
- Supply chain: Sigstore, SLSA, in-toto, TUF, SBOM generators, SCA tools
- Container: Kubescape, Cilium, SPIFFE/SPIRE, service meshes, runtime security
- Cloud: Oracle/Alibaba/Tencent cloud security, SASE vendors, CNAPP platforms
- CERTs: 24 national CERTs/CSIRTs (APAC, Europe, Africa, coordination bodies)
- Research: top-tier academic conferences, Pwn2Own, regional conferences
- Orgs: standards bodies (IETF/W3C/IEEE/ETSI), foundations (OpenSSF/CNCF),
  national cyber agencies (ANSSI/BSI/NCSC/CCCS/ACSC)
- AI: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025, NIST AI 600-1, EU AI Act, ISO 42001/23894,
  vendor AI safety frameworks (Anthropic RSP, Google SAIF, Microsoft RAI)
- Relationships: 15 cross-framework mappings (ATT&CK↔D3FEND, CCM↔ISO, etc.)
- Certifications: GIAC suite, CREST, practical certs (CRTO/CRTP/eCPPT)
- Bug bounty: Intigriti, Cobalt, Immunefi, HackenProof

Track HB: backward D=512 via D_SPLITS generalization in dQ+dKV kernels

- dQ backward: Qtile/dOtile/dQtile arrays [D_SPLITS], K/V/Q loaders
  in D_SPLITS loops; BD_HALF fixed at 128 (not BD/2)
- dKV backward: same pattern — dKtile/dVtile [D_SPLITS], inline
  RowLoader/KtLoader per chunk in Q/dO hoist, S, dV, dP, dK loops
- write D_SPLITS chunks with dh*BD_HALF column offsets
- D=512: 4 chunks of 128 cols; D=256 still 2 chunks (no regression)
- 350 tests pass (5 new TestD512Backward + 8 existing TestD512Forward)

commit.message:
Initial scaffold: PyClustering QuPath extension (Phase 1 MVP)

Python-powered clustering and phenotyping for highly multiplexed imaging data
via Appose embedded Python environment. Supports Leiden, KMeans, HDBSCAN,
Agglomerative, MiniBatchKMeans, and GMM clustering with UMAP/PCA/t-SNE
embedding and z-score/min-max/percentile normalization.

Java side: extension entry point, Appose service singleton, measurement
extraction from QuPath detections, result application (cluster labels as
PathClass classifications, embedding coordinates as measurements),
clustering configuration dialog with dynamic algorithm parameter panels.

Python side: pixi.toml (scikit-learn, scanpy, umap-learn, leidenalg --
no PyTorch/GPU required), init script with parent process watcher,
main clustering script, system info reporter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: qupath-extension-pyclustering
repository.description: Python-powered clustering and phenotyping for highly multiplexed imaging data in QuPath

commit.message:
Antibiotic resistance predictor for P. aeruginosa

Clinical web app that predicts antibiotic resistance using cross-resistance
patterns. Doctors select a target antibiotic, enter patient demographics and
known susceptibility results, and get a resistance probability estimate.

- RandomForest model (AUC 0.895, Accuracy 85.5%)
- 7 antibiotics: Cefepime, Ceftazidime, Imipenem, Levofloxacin, Meropenem,
  Piperacillin/Tazobactam, Ciprofloxacin
- Flask + Gunicorn, Render free tier ready
- Dark glassmorphism clinical UI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: remove build-test-windows from release dependency chain (#89)

build-tauri-windows and push-image both depended on
build-test-windows, which has persistent network failures on
CI runners. When it fails, the entire release pipeline gets
skipped even though build-test-windows was already removed
from the release job's direct needs.

- build-tauri-windows: depend on clippy-windows instead (validates compilation)
- push-image: depend only on test-linux (Docker image is Linux-only)

fix: persistent login + smart live updates (no jerk)

   (survives server restarts, 30-day expiry)
2. WebSocket: smart append — new messages appended to DOM
   instead of full re-render (no jerk/flicker)
3. Chat list: debounced refresh on new message
4. Polling fallback: 10s interval, uses smart append
5. Extracted _renderMessageBubble() for reuse

commit.message:
fix: Handle commit status entries in statusCheckRollup CI checks [Midtown !2121] (#1848)

* fix: Handle commit status entries in statusCheckRollup CI checks

GitHub's statusCheckRollup contains two types: check runs (with
`conclusion`) and commit statuses (with `state` instead). Services
like Codecov use commit statuses, so their entries had no `conclusion`
field — causing all_ci_checks_passed() and is_auto_mergeable() to
treat them as pending and block merges even when all checks passed.

Add resolve_check_status() helper that falls back to `state` when
`conclusion` is empty, normalizing both to uppercase for comparison.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Update detect_pr_issues() to use resolve_check_status()

detect_pr_issues() was still reading `conclusion` directly and only
checking for "FAILURE", missing commit status entries that use `state`
instead (e.g. Codecov) and "ERROR" status. Now uses the same
resolve_check_status() helper as is_auto_mergeable() and
all_ci_checks_passed().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Modularize prose_code.c into 12 modules + shared header

Split the monolithic 6,327-line prose_code.c into logically organized
modules following MODULARIZATION.md:

- prose_code.h: shared header with all types, macros, externs, prototypes
- buffer.c: arena allocator, gap buffer, line cache, wrap cache, undo stack
- theme.c: dark/light themes and DPI scaling
- spell.c: Windows ISpellChecker COM integration with static GUIDs
- syntax.c: C and Markdown tokenizers, keyword hash table
- document.c: document lifecycle, line/column math, gutter width
- editor.c: text editing ops, clipboard, undo/redo, cursor movement
- search.c: find/replace with match highlighting
- menu.c: menu definitions and action dispatch
- file_io.c: file I/O, atomic saves, autosave/crash recovery, tab management
- render.c: all GDI rendering (titlebar, tabs, editor, minimap, stats)
- wndproc.c: window procedure, input handling, bracket matching
- main.c: entry point, app icon, EditorState global

Also adds Makefile and GitHub Actions CI workflow for cross-compilation.

commit.message:
Correct false exponential moment theorem and prove Fatou bridge endpoint

Replace two mathematically false theorem statements:
1. gap_exponential_moment_geometric_bound (geometric decay impossible under
   probability measure — proved in test/proofideas_interaction_next_steps.lean)
2. gap_exp_interaction_uniform_bound with |V| (E[exp(p|V_κ|)] = ∞ for 4th
   Wiener chaos variables, since tails decay only as exp(-c√t))

The correct statement is gap_exp_neg_interaction_uniform_bound: the *negative*
exponential moments E[exp(-pV_κ)] are uniformly bounded in κ. This works
because :φ⁴: ≥ -6c² (right-skewed, bounded below), so exp(-V_κ) is controlled.
This is Nelson's bound (Simon Theorem V.14, Glimm-Jaffe Chapter 8.6).

Prove hasExpInteractionLp_of_analytic_inputs (sorry-free modulo its 2 inputs)
using the Fatou bridge from Part1Core, bypassing Part2/Part3 entirely.

Critical path reduced to 2 sorrys:
- gap_interactionCutoff_ae_convergence (a.e. UV convergence)
- gap_exp_neg_interaction_uniform_bound (Nelson's uniform bound)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
